name: Sabatier Catalyst Aging
description: Retroactive catalyst age accumulation from Sol 1. All ISRU operations since Sol 1 have contributed to catalyst and electrode degradation.
data_sources: NASA Sabatier reactor studies: Ni catalyst life ~2000 hours
ISS Oxygen Generation System: PEM electrode target life 70,000 hours
MOXIE Mars 2020: Solid oxide electrolyzer degradation data

notes: Catalyst degrades much faster than electrodes (2,000 vs 70,000 hour life)
By Sol 400, catalyst is only 50% efficient, needs replacement
By Sol 700, catalyst is at minimum 20% efficiency
Electrodes remain highly efficient throughout mission duration
This creates strategic tension: run ISRU early (fresh catalyst) vs late (better infrastructure)

strategic_implications: Early aggressive ISRU maximizes catalyst efficiency but risks power/infrastructure problems
Late conservative ISRU has mature infrastructure but severely degraded catalysts
Optimal strategy likely involves catalyst replacement planning around Sol 400-500
Governors that ignore catalyst aging will see production plummet after Sol 400
v7 makes 'ISRU plant' module placement timing much more critical
